content.full_text:
P.O. BOX 637 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95314

Technical Assistance/Administration
(916) 322-5563

Executive/Legal
322-5521

Enforcement/Conflict of Interest
322-6441

May 7, 1976

Mr. Harold W. Trott
Real Estate Appraiser
P.O. Box 203
Eureka CA 95501

Re: Request for Opinion
    No. 76-057

Dear Mr. Trott:

Thank you for your letter of April 20, 1976, requesting an opinion with respect to whether the activities in which you are engaged involve a conflict of interest within the meaning of the Political Reform Act. Because your request does not raise a substantial question of interpretation under the Act, no formal opinion will be issued. However, I hope the following informal comments will be helpful.

Based on the facts outlined in your letter, your only involvement with the Political Reform Act would arise by reason of your employment as an appraiser by the County of Humboldt. Although you currently are not a member, officer or employee of the county, you may be concerned with whether you are a "consultant" to the county within the meaning of the Political Reform Act.

The Commission has adopted a regulation which excludes from the definition of the term "consultant" any person who conducts research and arrives at conclusions independent of the control and direction of the government agency by which he is employed and possesses no authority with respect to any decision of the government agency beyond the rendition of information, advice, recommendation or counsel. 2 Cal. Adm. Code §18700(a)(2). As an independent real estate appraiser who has contracted with the county to perform certain services, you fall within this exclusion. Accordingly, you are not a public official within the meaning of the Act and would not, by reason of your employment by the County of Humboldt and your relationship with Mr. Mathews, have a conflict of interest.

Mr. Harold Trott
May 7, 1976
Page Two

If you wish to appeal the denial of your opinion request,
you may do so pursuant to 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18321. If
you have any further questions, please contact Kenneth Finnery,
Chief of the Commission's Legal Division at (916) 322-5901.

Very truly yours,

[signature]

Michael Bennett
Executive Director

MB:pvp

HAROLD W. TROTT

REAL ESTATE APPRAISALS

MEMBER AMERICAN INSTITUTE OF REAL ESTATE APPRAISERS
P.O. BOX 203     EUREKA, CALIFORNIA 95501
PHONE 442-7397

April 20, 1976

Fair Political Practices Commission,
1100 "K" Street,
Sacramento, California - 95814

Dear Sirs:

     I am an independent real estate appraiser.

     Under date of April 10, 1974, I submitted an appraisal report to Francis B. Mathews, Attorney at Law, which detailed my estimate of damages to property of Jack Allen at McCann, California. The Allen property is a 152 acre summer home site with adjacent timber land. Damages occurred by reason of a sloppy logging job upstream on a small creek which runs through the Allen property on its way to the Eel River. Debris in the way of logs, limbs, gravel, and silt was deposited on the Allen property and grains within, and under Allen buildings. The deposit of silt and gravel was deep enough to kill the orchard trees.

     Through the summer of 1974 I became aware that other appraisers were working the case for the logger and his insurance company. Then, nothing happened throughout all of 1975. I assumed that the case had settled out.

     In March of 1976, a Deputy in the Office of the County Counsel hired me for a case known as "South Bay Marina v. County of Humboldt". This is an inverse condemnation case. The owners of South Bay Marina maintain that faulty construction of a County of Humboldt culvert has caused the basin at South Bay Marina to fill with silt, and they submit that there are other damages attributable to erosion.

     I am in both cases only as to before and after value of the properties. Proximate cause of damage is outside of my field.

     It turns out that the Allen case has not settled.

     It also turns out that Francis B. Mathews - the attorney for whom I am working on the Allen case - is one of the owners of South Bay Marina.

     Do I have a conflict of interest within the meaning of the act which you people enforce?

     The Humboldt County Counsel suggested that I phone your office. Mr. Robert Stern, of your office, suggested that I put it in writing.

Page...1...

Mr. Matthews - when contacted yesterday - said that he could care less. I have worked for Mr. Matthews off and on many times over the past fifteen years.

The County Counsel is concerned only as to the application of the act which you people enforce. According to the wording of the act, I appear to be a "consultant" to the County of Humboldt in the South Bay Marine case.

Right now, I have no money coming from the Allen case. I was paid for my investigation and for my appraisal report back in May of 1974. But, should the Allen case come to trial, I will be called upon to appear as a witness in defense of my damage estimate. There is a continuing obligation to Mr. Matthews.

Sincerely yours,

Harold W. Trott
M.A.I.

Page...2..
